meta.title: Box 3 & the Courts | Legal cases and rulings
meta.description: Overview of the key court rulings on Box 3 tax in the Netherlands: the Christmas ruling 2021, mass objection, remediation legislation and the new system.
meta.keywords: box 3 court case, box 3 ruling, christmas ruling box 3, mass objection box 3, box 3 supreme court netherlands, box 3 new system 2027

hero.title: Box 3 & the Courts
hero.subtitle: From the Christmas ruling in 2021 to the new system in 2027. Everything you need to know about the legal battles around Box 3.

context.title: What is going on exactly?
context.p1: The Dutch Box 3 system calculated wealth tax for years based on a deemed return, regardless of what you actually earned. The Supreme Court ruled that this violated property rights and the prohibition of discrimination under the European Convention on Human Rights (ECHR).
context.p2: Since then, the government has introduced temporary legislation and is developing a completely new system based on actual returns. For taxpayers who overpaid, remediation arrangements are in place.

timeline.label: Christmas ruling (Supreme Court)
timeline.summary: On 24 December 2021 the Supreme Court ruled that the deemed-return system of Box 3 is incompatible with European law. Taxpayers who had filed an objection were entitled to remediation.

timeline.label: Mass objection & remediation
timeline.summary: The tax authority accepted the ruling and set up a mass-objection process. Millions of assessments were revised. Taxpayers who had not filed an objection were initially excluded.

timeline.label: Bridging legislation
timeline.summary: As developing a new system takes time, the government introduced bridging legislation. Box 3 is now temporarily calculated using actual asset categories rather than a single deemed return.

timeline.label: Non-objectors: rulings
timeline.summary: The Supreme Court and lower courts considered whether non-objectors are also entitled to remediation. In several cases the courts ruled this was not automatic, though individual proceedings remained possible.

timeline.label: New Box 3 system in preparation
timeline.summary: The government is developing a new system based on actual returns, intended to take effect in 2027. Until then the bridging legislation remains in force.

items.title: Check your assessments
items.body: Check whether you filed a timely objection for the years 2017 to 2022. Taxpayers who were part of the mass-objection process are entitled to remediation.

items.title: Calculate your actual return
items.body: Use our calculator to see what you pay under the current bridging legislation, and compare it to earlier years.

items.title: Consult a tax advisor
items.body: The Box 3 situation is complex and depends on your personal circumstances. A tax advisor can help you with objection procedures or optimisation.
